candidate_summary: HARUSPEX-EVAL: frozen K-zone rubric template + constrained-decoding K-tuple judge + lookup-table aggregation (separation of per-zone judgment from non-learned aggregation).
rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.note: Direct twin: DAGMetric deterministic rubric + analytic per-criterion scoring already covers frozen-template + per-zone judgment + deterministic aggregation.
DAGMetric provides deterministic-control eval with fixed decision graph + per-node criteria; lookup-table aggregation is the standard DAGMetric pattern.
Rubric Is All You Need: structured rubric-based eval; differs only in question-specific vs. frozen template (degree, not kind).
SLOT structured-output via constrained decoding — same K-tuple emission primitive.
Structured-output benchmark — constrained K-tuple emission is the dominant primitive.
Grammar-constrained decoding — exact match for the constrained-decoding mechanism in K-tuple emission.
Deterministic-output primitive in LLM workflow — overlaps lookup-table/deterministic aggregation claim.

fail_reason: 7 of 8 results hit (sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7). The candidate's three primitives are each prior art: (a) frozen-template rubric eval is exemplified by DAGMetric (DeepEval) and DAG-based deterministic rubric pipelines; (b) constrained-decoding K-tuple emission is covered by SLOT, grammar-constrained decoding, and the structured-output benchmark; (c) non-learned deterministic aggregation over per-criterion scores is the standard DAGMetric/analytic-rubric pattern. The claimed novelty (frozen K-zone + constrained K-tuple + lookup-table aggregation) is a recomposition of well-established eval primitives — no novel mechanism beyond the haruspex-themed framing. Independent cross-agent score agrees with prior 07_hit_miss.json: FAIL.
